title: Extend bee-write-guard.mjs with CLI-shape validation (4th check)
action: PREREQUISITE (validating iteration 1, Blocker 4): hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs imports shared lib modules dynamically from the VENDORED copy (.bee/bin/lib/<name>, via libModuleUrl), never from the template tree directly - so before writing or testing this cell's change, run `node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply` once to vendor cell harness-integration-1's validate-args.mjs into .bee/bin/lib/ (this is generic, automatic, and safe to re-run per cell harness-integration-4's own finding - no code change needed for the vendoring itself). Then add a 4th check to bee-write-guard.mjs's existing three-check pass (gate guard, reservation guard, privacy/scout guard): when a Bash tool call is shaped like a bee.mjs or legacy bee_*.mjs invocation, parse its command+args and run them through validate-args.mjs (imported the same way main() already imports guards.mjs, via libModuleUrl) against the registry from cell harness-integration-1; on mismatch, deny (exit 2) with the same structured correction the dispatcher itself would give, before the shell executes it. CRITICAL (validating iteration 1, Blocker 3): main()'s existing checks all share ONE try/catch and ONE `denial` variable - the new check's logic must be written so it can NEVER overwrite or discard a denial already set by checks 1-3, whether via an early unconditional assignment, a wrong early-return, or an uncaught exception in the new Bash-parsing logic (which the shared catch would turn into a silent fail-open for ALL FOUR checks, not just the new one). Write skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs as an end-to-end integration test that spawns hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs itself (not just guards.mjs's exported functions - test_lib.mjs's existing tests cannot reach this file's own dispatch logic) with crafted stdin JSON payloads, asserting: (a) a malformed bee.mjs-shaped call is denied, (b) the three existing checks still deny/allow exactly as before this cell for their own test cases, (c) a case where the new check's parsing logic is forced to throw does NOT erase a denial already computed by an existing check in the same call. This check must be strictly additive - appended after the three existing checks, never modifying their logic (per D4, the highest-risk file in this slice). Capture the before-state (a malformed bee.mjs-shaped call is NOT denied by write-guard today, only gate/reservation/privacy checks apply) as this cell's red_failure_evidence at cap time.

truths: a malformed bee.mjs-shaped Bash call is denied before it executes, with a structured correction on stderr

the existing gate guard, reservation guard, and privacy/scout guard behave exactly as before this cell - zero regression, proven by test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs spawning the actual hook script, not just by re-running test_lib.mjs

the new check's logic never overwrites or discards a denial already computed by checks 1-3 in the same invocation - proven by a test that forces the new check to throw while a prior check has already denied, asserting the original deny still wins

artifacts.substantive: contains the new 4th check as an isolated, additive block that cannot clobber an existing denial
spawns the real hook script with crafted stdin, not a stub

key_links: the new check imports validate-args.mjs the same way (libModuleUrl against the vendored .bee/bin/lib/ copy) main() already imports guards.mjs
notes: The prerequisite onboarding-vendoring step touches .bee/bin/lib/validate-args.mjs and .bee/onboarding.json (both git-tracked, generated/managed files) outside this cell's declared `files` array - record both in trace.files_changed at cap time for disclosure completeness, even though they are not hand-edited (validating iteration 2, informational note).
prohibitions: No change whatsoever to the existing gate-guard, reservation-guard, or privacy/scout-guard logic in this file - this is the single highest-risk prohibition in the entire feature

trace.outcome: Extended hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs with an additive 4th check (CLI-shape validation, D4): a Bash call shaped like a bee.mjs/bee_*.mjs invocation is parsed and validated against the vendored command-registry.mjs/validate-args.mjs; on schema mismatch it denies with a structured correction. The new check runs unconditionally for Bash calls but can only ASSIGN denial when none exists yet, and its own try/catch is separate from the shared outer catch, so it can never overwrite or discard a denial from checks 1-3 even when forced to throw. Wrote test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs (15 checks) spawning the real hook script end-to-end: malformed-call denial, well-formed-call allow, all three pre-existing checks' deny/allow paths unchanged, and the forced-throw-preserves-prior-denial scenario. Ran the onboarding-vendoring prerequisite first (validating iteration 1 Blocker 4) to populate .bee/bin/lib/validate-args.mjs + command-registry.mjs. Zero regression: test_lib.mjs 124/124 and test_bee_cli.mjs 32/32 still passing.
trace.verify_output: test_lib.mjs: 124 passed, 0 failed; test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs: 15 passed, 0 failed; exit 0
